sanskrit:
न तदस्ति पृथिव्यां वा दिवि देवेषु वा पुनः।
सत्त्वं प्रकृतिजैर्मुक्तं यदेभिः स्यात्त्रिभिर्गुणैः॥
transliteration: na tad asti pṛthivyāṃ vā divi deveṣu vā punaḥ | sattvaṃ prakṛtijair muktaṃ yad ebhiḥ syāt tribhir guṇaiḥ ||
literal_meaning: na—not; tat—that; asti—there is; pṛthivyām—on earth; vā—or; divi—in heaven; deveṣu—among gods; vā—or; punaḥ—again; sattvam—existence; prakṛti-jaiḥ—born of material nature; muktam—freed; yat—which; ebhiḥ—from these; syāt—may be; tribhiḥ—three; guṇaiḥ—qualities.
contextual_meaning: Every sentient being—terrestrial or celestial—is woven into the same psychological matrix of three temperamental forces (guṇas). No one stands outside this field; liberation is not escape but conscious participation.
psychological_state: Radical acceptance of embeddedness—an ego-deflating reminder that no role, rank, or ritual removes the shared human substrate of conditioning.
trigger_scenario: A high-performing executive meditates daily yet still snaps at her child; realizing the guṇas operate even in ‘spiritual’ people dissolves perfectionistic defenses.
anchor_text: No one escapes the three moods; freedom is dancing with them wide-awake.
